task: discover FlowLink, create + pay an invoice for 10 USDC
constraints: no browser; may read all .md files at /llms.txt and /skills/* and /.well-known/*

steps.thinking: I have nothing but a base URL. Convention says agent-aware sites publish /llms.txt as a discovery index — that's the cheapest first probe. If it 404s I'd fall back to /.well-known/. If it returns text, I get the entire surface for free.
steps.result_summary: FlowLink is a compliance-first payment layer on HashKey Chain id 133. Index lists 5 skill files (invoice, pay, compliance, receipt, reputation), confirms SIWE auth via /v1/auth/siwe/{nonce,verify}, requires Idempotency-Key on writes, errors are RFC 9457 problem+json with code+agent_action, settlement chain 133 RPC noted.
steps.next_decision: Pull invoice.md and pay.md in one parallel batch to get exact request/response shapes, and generate two test wallets (payer + receiver) at the same time.

steps.thinking: Need the contract for invoice creation and payment, plus deterministic wallets. These are independent so I run them concurrently in one Bash message. The skill markdown files are the actual API spec since OpenAPI ships in v0.2.
steps.result_summary: invoice.md gives POST /v1/invoices schema with receiver_address/amount/token/purpose/due_at, returns invoice_id+status+chain_id 133. pay.md gives POST /v1/pay with invoice_id+payer_address+token, returns transaction_id+status+receipt_id+compliance block. Confirmed scopes: invoice:write, pay:execute. Errors are agent-actionable (compliance_blocked_sanctions = STOP, idempotency_conflict = fresh key).
steps.next_decision: I now have the full happy path. Hit the SIWE nonce endpoint to start auth.

steps.thinking: Generated payer 0xe454...5604 and receiver 0xB3CC...f91b in parallel with the skill fetch — viem privateKeyToAccount gives EIP-55 addresses straight away. This was step 2's parallel sibling, so it doesn't add a wall-clock round trip.
steps.result_summary: payer 0xe454102D979A574291Ff221eF3085c3956FF5604, receiver 0xB3CC41E96749C9C4E80ba9D257a83A8DFe59f91b. Private keys captured for SIWE signing.
steps.next_decision: Request SIWE nonce for the payer address — chain_id 133 per llms.txt.

steps.thinking: POST /v1/auth/siwe/nonce with the payer address and chain_id 133. Server should return a fully-formed EIP-4361 message I just sign verbatim — I don't have to construct the SIWE string myself, which avoids domain/URI mismatch bugs.
steps.result_summary: Got nonce WeZbqZULSlk9koZR0 and a complete EIP-4361 message with 5 min expiration. Server pre-builds the message — I just sign it. This is exactly the pattern pay.md hinted at.
steps.next_decision: Sign the message with viem inline, then POST to /verify in the same shell call to keep latency low.

steps.thinking: Combine the sign + verify in one shell command. viem's signMessage returns a hex sig; I shove it into the verify body. If signature recovery fails the server returns 4xx and I'd need to re-fetch the nonce. Keep \n in the message exactly as returned — SIWE is brittle to whitespace.
steps.result_summary: Got JWT (EdDSA, kid flk-jwt-2026-04) with all 6 scopes: invoice:read/write, pay:execute, receipt:read, compliance:check, reputation:read. expires_in 3600s. Subject cmoasb8lm0001cp4f7fxm2v11.
steps.next_decision: Per pay.md guidance, preflight the receiver via /v1/compliance/check before creating the invoice — cheaper than an invoice creation that gets blocked at pay time.

steps.thinking: compliance.md (linked from the index) says check returns sanctions_ok + score + velocity. If the receiver were OFAC-sanctioned I'd abort here, never even creating an invoice — saves a write and a failed pay. Pure read.
steps.result_summary: Receiver clean: sanctions_ok=true, score=100, velocity 0/$10000 USD in 24h. Sources ofac-api + velocity-24h. Safe to invoice.
steps.next_decision: Create the $10 USDC invoice with a fresh Idempotency-Key.

steps.thinking: POST /v1/invoices with receiver, amount 10.00, token USDC, a human-readable purpose. Idempotency-Key = uuidgen so a transient retry won't duplicate. Skipping due_at — defaults to 30 days per spec.
steps.result_summary: Invoice inv_01KPVY6KJBM71JBKZ8FPM71JBK created, status pending, chain_id 133, due_at 2026-05-23. flowlink_id form is flowlink:inv/<id> (URI scheme).
steps.next_decision: Pay it. Same JWT, fresh Idempotency-Key, payer_address must match the SIWE-authenticated address.

steps.thinking: POST /v1/pay with invoice_id + payer_address + token. The 202 response will tell me whether it routed via HSP mandate (checkout_url) or settled inline. On a testnet with hsp_configured=false I'd expect inline settlement.
steps.result_summary: transaction_id txn_01KPVY6Z3GZH25C187MYZH25C1, status=settled (no HSP mandate — hsp_configured:false on this dev box, so it shortcuts to settled), receipt_id rcp_01KPVY6Z45WPEVESMRXRWPEVES, compliance score 100. Done in 8 calls.
steps.next_decision: Task complete. Write the JSON log.

outcome_summary: Discovered FlowLink via /llms.txt, learned the API entirely from /skills/*.md, completed SIWE auth, compliance-screened the receiver, created and paid a $10 USDC invoice — all in 8 tool calls, no SDK, no docs other than the agent-served markdown.
